lesson_contract.objective: Build capability in your current role through deliberate practice, useful feedback, coachability, and reliable execution.
objective_points: Use a practice and feedback loop
Connect capability to current role expectations
Confirm any increase in ownership with your manager and current written role plan
lesson_contract.transition_spoken_text: Take the Career Growth Path checkpoint next. Then complete the Mission Control and Growth Capstone.

forbidden_language_removals.exact_source_language: hitting your numbers for 90-plus days, you move up
forbidden_language_removals.replacement_rule: Use current written role expectations and never promise advancement on a fixed timeline.

forbidden_language_removals.exact_source_language: The earning potential is significantly higher because you're earning commission
forbidden_language_removals.replacement_rule: Remove compensation comparisons and point learners to current written sources.

forbidden_language_removals.exact_source_language: Some people advance in six months. Some take a year.
forbidden_language_removals.replacement_rule: Remove example promotion timelines from reusable training.
Do not promise a role ladder or named advancement path.

scenes.title: Build capability first
scenes.spoken_text: Development starts with getting better at the work assigned to you now. Choose a skill that matters, practice it, and use it in real work. Then look at the result. The aim is to work reliably and leave the record or handoff ready for the next person.

visual_plan.shot: Andrea standing in a medium shot
visual_plan.editor_note: Open on current capability rather than a career ladder.

scenes.title: Use a feedback loop
scenes.spoken_text: Ask for specific feedback and listen before you defend your first approach. If you do not understand the feedback, ask a direct question. Practice the new approach, use it in the work, and bring back an example of what changed.

visual_plan.shot: Andrea seated with a single feedback card
visual_plan.editor_note: Optional cutaway shows practice, application, and review without time promises.

scenes.title: Confirm ownership changes
scenes.spoken_text: Getting better at the work does not automatically change your responsibilities. Take on more ownership after your manager confirms it and the change appears in your current written role plan. A training example or another person's duties cannot redefine your work.

visual_plan.shot: Andrea beside a closed written-plan folder
visual_plan.editor_note: Do not show a promotion arrow, hierarchy, or title.

scenes.title: Use current written expectations
scenes.spoken_text: Use your current role sheet, offer letter, and manager direction to understand the work assigned to you. Pick a skill that supports those expectations. If an older training example says something different, follow the current documents and ask your manager.

visual_plan.shot: Andrea at a desk with two generic current-document folders
visual_plan.editor_note: Keep the documents free of pay figures and role titles.

scenes.title: Make development concrete
scenes.spoken_text: A useful development conversation stays close to the work. Talk about the expectation you are working toward, the skill you are practicing, and the feedback you are applying. If the conversation includes new ownership, get that change confirmed in writing.

visual_plan.shot: Andrea seated in a conversational medium shot
visual_plan.editor_note: Use a calm coaching posture with no advancement promise.

scenes.title: Continue into the capstone
scenes.spoken_text: Take the Career Growth Path checkpoint next. Then complete the Mission Control and Growth Capstone.

visual_plan.shot: Andrea centered in a medium shot

edit_operations.source_problem: The source presents a company-wide role ladder and fixed advancement framing rather than reusable capability growth.
The source ties moving up to a fixed performance window and named next level.
The source promises higher earnings, commissions, a management path, and compensation tied to team output.
The source names example promotion timelines and turns fixed numeric performance into advancement criteria.
The source combines useful coachability ideas with revenue tests and first-consideration promises.
The source closes with a broad growth assurance instead of the actual checkpoint and capstone sequence.
